stories.title: Trump says U.S. will 'guide' stranded ships through the Strait. And, key Senate races to watch
stories.description: President Trump announced that the U.S will "guide" stranded ships through the Strait of Hormuz. And, Democrats face an uphill battle to gain Senate control. Here are the key races to watch.

stories.title: Trump says the U.S. will 'guide' stranded ships from the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The United States will launch an effort on Monday to "guide" stranded ships from the Iran-gripped Strait of Hormuz, President Donald Trump said, as two ships around the strait reported attacks.

stories.title: Gas prices went up more than 30 cents a gallon last week. How high could they go?
stories.description: U.S. gas prices were nearly $3 an average prior to the start of the war in Iran.

stories.title: Modern economic chokepoints in war and the impact on geopolitics
stories.description: Hoover Institution fellow Eyck Freymann explains what the Iran war and the blockade of the Straight of Hormuz reveals about modern economic chokepoints, geopolitics and war strategies.

stories.title: Israeli attacks kill dozens within days in Lebanon despite ceasefire
stories.description: The health ministry says 77 people have been killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon since Thursday.

stories.title: Some Iranians fear the regime is now more entrenched - and ready for revenge
stories.description: Ordinary Iranians tell of worry about increased repression by authorities after the war is over.

stories.title: Thirteen killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon, health ministry says
stories.description: Four women and a child are among the dead, as fighting between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah continues despite a ceasefire.

stories.title: British couple jailed in Iran: 'We're likely to be here for a long time'
stories.description: Lindsay and Craig Foreman are facing the reality of a 10-year prison sentence following their arrest in Iran while on a motorcycle tour last year.

stories.title: Israeli 'double-tap' strike kills three rescue workers in Lebanon, officials say
stories.description: They are among nine people killed in successive attacks on Tuesday, which Israel's military says targeted a Hezbollah commander.

stories.title: Former US officials criticise Pentagon silence on deadly Iran school attack
stories.description: In the two months since the deadly strike, the US defence department has said only that the incident is under investigation.

stories.title: Who is making decisions in Iran?
stories.description: Iran's new supreme leader is supposed to have the final say, but in practice the picture is far murkier.

stories.title: Why and how is US blockading Iranian ports in Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Donald Trump says that the US is blockading the Strait of Hormuz. What does this mean in practice?

stories.title: Watch: There's a ceasefire in Lebanon - but the fighting hasn't stopped
stories.description: Israel and Hezbollah are still fighting in Lebanon despite a US-brokered ceasefire that began 12 days ago.

stories.title: Did Trump save eight Iranian women from execution?
stories.description: The US president says he saved eight Iranian women from execution, but Iran says otherwise.

stories.title: NATO chief says Europeans have 'gotten message' from Trump on defence
stories.description: Europeans have "heard" US President Donald Trump's message of frustration over the Iran war and are "stepping up", NATO chief Mark Rutte said on Monday after Washington announced it would withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany. "European leaders have gotten the message," Rutte said b...

stories.title: Hezbollah deploys hard-to-jam fibre optic drones, shifting battlefield tactics
stories.description: Back in March, Lebanon was pulled back into conflict amid escalating regional tensions linked to Iran, as Hezbollah fired rockets into Israel. Israel responded with waves of air strikes and later ground operations in the south, displacing hundreds of thousands of people. While ea...

stories.title: US to 'guide' stranded ships from Hormuz: What we know about 'Project Freedom'
stories.description: The United States said Monday it is ready to “guide” commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz in a new effort to end the blockade wreaking havoc on the global economy. The U.S.-led Joint Maritime Information Center advised ships to cross the strait in Oman’s waters, saying i...

stories.title: Trump says US operation will aid ships stranded in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: President Donald Trump said on Sunday the U.S. would start an effort on Monday morning to free ships stranded in the Strait ​of Hormuz as a "humanitarian gesture" to aid neutral countries in the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran. Trump provided few details about the plan, which he said ...

stories.title: War preparations under way in Iran as Hormuz tensions with US escalate
stories.description: Amid stalled talks and sparring over key waterway, Iranian media say US warship struck, but Washington denies incident.

stories.title: Winning peace in Lebanon is harder than winning war
stories.description: Resolving the conflict in Lebanon necessitates taking into account its long history and new geopolitical realities.

stories.title: Iran says it fired missiles at US warship to prevent it entering Hormuz
stories.description: Iranian state media says two missiles have struck a US navy destroyer to prevent it entering the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: NATO chief says Europeans have ‘gotten the message’ from Trump on defence
stories.description: The US president has accused some NATO countries of not doing enough to support the US-Israel war on Iran.

stories.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 66 as Trump announces Hormuz mission?
stories.description: Trump orders new Hormuz mission after Iran says it receives US response to its peace proposal.

stories.title: Japan PM: Hormuz closure ‘inflicting enormous impact’ on Asia Pacific
stories.description: Japan’s PM says the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz is ‘inflicting an ‘enormous impact’ on Asia Pacific.

stories.title: Iran warns US to stay out of Hormuz after Trump says US will ‘guide’ ships
stories.description: President Trump says the US will begin 'Project Freedom' on Monday to guide stranded ships through Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Crew of Iranian ship seized by US forces evacuated to Pakistan, gov’t says
stories.description: Twenty-two crew members from the seized Iranian ship MV Touska have been transferred to Pakistan for repatriation.

stories.title: FO says 22 crew members on Iranian ship seized by US have been evacuated to Pakistan
stories.description: The Foreign Office (FO) said on Monday that as a “confidence-building measure” by the United States, 22 crew members on an Iranian ship that was seized by US forces had been evacuated to Pakistan. The statement came hours after American outlet ABC News reported that an Iranian sh...

stories.title: 7 dead in Lebanon by Israeli ‘ceasefire violations’
stories.description: BEIRUT: Fresh Israeli attacks killed at least seven people in southern Lebanon on Sunday despite an ongoing ceasefire, Lebanese media reported, as Israel continued to attack the country despite ceasefire. In an airstrike in the town of Safad al-Battikh, three people were killed w...

stories.title: Jerusalem Christians worried after attack on nun by Jewish extremist
stories.description: JERUSALEM: Footage of an attack on a Catholic nun in Jerusalem shocked the world when it went viral this week, but for worshippers attending Sunday mass at Saint Stephen’s Basilica, it was just the latest example of mounting religious hostility. The attack on Tuesday was captured...

stories.title: Iran reviewing US response to 14-point plan; Trump says Washington having 'very positive' discussions with Tehran
stories.description: US President Donald Trump said on Monday that his representatives were having “very positive discussions” with Iran that could “lead to something very positive for all”. He made these remarks in a Truth Social Post, also announcing an initiative to guide vessels stuck in the Stra...

stories.title: US Secretary of State Rubio to visit Vatican, Rome after Trump's row with Pope
stories.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio will visit Rome and the Vatican this week, an Italian government source said on Sunday, just weeks after a clash between Donald Trump and Pope Leo. Rubio, who is a Catholic, is expected to meet Vatican Secretary of State Pietro Parolin and Italia...

stories.title: Iran says ball in US court on war or diplomacy
stories.description: WASHINGTON: A man stands atop the Frederick Douglass Memorial Bridge on Saturday to protest the US-Israeli war on Iran. Guido Reichstadter climbed the bridge in Washington on May Day, stayed there overnight, and posted a sunrise photo from one of the bridge’s arches while calling...

stories.title: Missiles fired at U.S. frigate in Strait of Hormuz, claims Iranian media
stories.description: No U.S. ​Navy ⁠ships have been ‌struck, ‌U.S. ‌Central ⁠Command ⁠said on ​May 4, ‌after Iran asserted that it ‌had ​forced a ⁠U.S. ‌warship to turn ‌back

stories.title: Pakistan Foreign Minister discusses regional situation with Iranian counterpart
stories.description: Mr. Dar, who is also the Deputy Prime Minister, reaffirmed Pakistan’s commitment to promoting constructive engagement, underscoring that dialogue and diplomacy remain the only viable path toward peaceful resolution of issues and achieving lasting peace

stories.title: Dubai airport passenger traffic drops 66% over war
stories.description: Dubai International was targeted several times by drones as the oil-rich UAE bore the brunt of Iran's retaliation for U.S.-Israeli strikes.

stories.title: Iran military says U.S. forces will be attacked if they enter Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Any American interference in the new maritime regime of the Strait of Hormuz will be considered a violation of the ceasefire, says Iranian parliament's national security commission

stories.title: Trump announces ‘Project Freedom’ to help stranded ships leave Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: “Project Freedom” would begin on Monday (May 4, 2026) morning in West Asia, Mr. Trump said, adding that his representatives are having discussions with Iran that could lead to something “very positive for all”

stories.title: Tanker hit by 'unknown projectiles' off UAE coast: U.K. maritime agency
stories.description: The report came as the U.S. and Iran remain deadlocked in peace negotiations since a ceasefire in the West Asian war came into effect on April 8

stories.title: Respect-based dialogue needed to develop Russia-Japan relations — senior Russian MP
stories.description: According to Grigory Karasin, Russian lawmakers share views that "the sanctions pressure on Russia is senseless"

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz fully controlled by US — treasury secretary
stories.description: US has blockaded the ships going into or out of Iranian ports, Scott Bessent said
